channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said that he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: 4 American soldiers missing from training area in Lithuania, US military says
item.description: A search is underway in Lithuania for four U.S. soldiers who went missing from a training ground during scheduled tactical training, the U.S. military said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders more evacuations and Palestinians stage rare anti-Hamas protests
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas after breaking the ceasefire last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US soldiers missing in Lithuania: Pentagon
item.description: The Army said Wednesday that four U.S. soldiers are missing in Lithuania. The U.S. Army Europe and Africa said in a press release that the missing soldiers, part of the Army’s 1st Brigade, 3rd Infantry Division, were close to the Lithuanian city of Pabradė within a training area. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of breaking ceasefire terms protecting energy sites
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have accused each other of breaking the terms of a tentative U.S-brokered deal to pause strikes on energy infrastructure, underscoring the challenges to negotiating a broader peace in the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 24 dead as wildfires ravage southern South Korea and force thousands to evacuate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Wind-driven wildfires that were among South Korea’s worst ever have ravaged the country’s southern regions, killing 24 people, destroying more than 300 structures and forcing 28,800 residents to evacuate, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Niger’s junta leader cements grip on power as he is sworn in as president for a 5-year period
item.description: NIAMEY, Niger (AP) — Niger’s junta leader, Abdourahamane Tchiani, was sworn in Wednesday as the country’s president for a transition period of five years under a new charter that replaces the West African nation’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: DALATOU MAMANE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: His brother’s keeper: Ilay David warns his brother, Hamas hostage Evyatar, is running out of time
item.description: Evyatar David's brother, Ilay, is fighting just to play music together again. For almost a year and a half, Evyatar has been held hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Accuser tells a trial that actor Gérard Depardieu groped her bottom and breasts on film set
item.description: A woman accusing French actor Gérard Depardieu of sexual assault has told a landmark trial in Paris that he groped her buttocks and her breasts several times on a film set.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Captured Ukrainians convicted on terrorism charges by Russia in what Kyiv called a sham trial
item.description: Russia has convicted 23 captured Ukrainians on terrorism charges stemming from the war in Ukraine in a trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Deeply unpopular Peruvian president calls for general elections in April 2026
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peruvian President Dina Boluarte said Tuesday the country will hold general elections one year from now in an effort to end years of instability. The deeply unpopular Boularte said the April 2026 polls will elect a new president, 130 deputies and 60 senators.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds in Gaza take to streets to protest Hamas
item.description: Hundreds of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip took to the streets Tuesday for a rare protest against the Palestinian militant group Hamas, which has controlled the war-torn strip for nearly two decades.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: US says it brokered deal to end fighting in the Black Sea in talks with Ukraine and Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The United States said Tuesday that it had reached a tentative agreement for Ukraine and Russia to stop fighting and ensure safe navigation in the Black Sea in separate talks with both sides, but many details were unresolved, and the Kremlin made the deal conditional on lifting
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: America's allies alarmed by a leaked group chat about attack plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As wake-up calls go, the alarms don’t get much louder. Allies of the United States see the group chat between top U.S.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, EMMA BURROWS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s Supreme Court ends first day of Bolsonaro coup proceedings without a decision
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A panel of Brazil’s Supreme Court justices wrapped up a first day of proceedings Tuesday to determine whether former President Jair Bolsonaro and close allies will stand trial on five counts, including attempting to stage a coup, without reaching a decision.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says US to ease sanctions as part of targeted ceasefire deal with Ukraine
item.description: Editor's note: This article has been updated to more accurately describe the exclusion of Russian banks from the SWIFT system. Russia said Monday that it expects the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis' doctors considered ending treatment, said 'there was a real risk he might not make it': report
item.description: Doctors taking care of Pope Francis weighed ending their treatment at one point as there “was a risk that he would not make it," a report said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Africa president says persecution of whites a 'false narrative' as Musk repeats genocide claim
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa said Monday the claim that white people are being persecuted in his country is a “completely false narrative.” It was his latest attempt to push back against allegations made by U.S.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil apologizes to families of victims of military dictatorship's mass grave
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil’s government on Monday apologized to families of victims of the country’s military dictatorship whose remains could be among those found in a clandestine mass grave 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey detains journalists as protests grow over the jailing of key Erdogan rival
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — A media union said Turkish authorities arrested several journalists at their homes in a crackdown Monday, amid growing protests over the jailing of Istanbul’s mayor, a top rival to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN blames Israelis for attack on compound but doesn't mention Hamas, says forced to reduce Gaza footprint
item.description: In spite of continued humanitarian need in Gaza conflict zone, the U.N. announced it was reducing its footprint, accusing Israel of targeting its personnel. Israel Defense Forces deny claim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US peace talks with Ukraine, Russia get underway in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Discussions between U.S. and Russian delegations aimed at ending the war in Ukraine are underway Monday in Saudi Arabia, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Finding God in Gaza: Former Israeli hostage found faith to help her survive Hamas terror
item.description: Twenty-year-old former hostage Agam Berger survived 482 days in Gaza held by Hamas terrorists. Berger has found a new importance in religion, which kept her going during her captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heathrow says some flights will resume after a fire cut power to Europe's busiest airport
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heathrow Airport said it planned to resume some flights Friday after a large fire at an electrical substation knocked out power to Europe’s busiest flight hub and disrupted global travel for hundreds of thousands of passengers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, DANICA KIRKA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: European countries warn against US travel amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The UK and Germany updated travel advisories after citizens were arrested and detained when attempting to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: BTS is recording new album this summer: Exclusive
item.description: Jin and J-Hope have finished their military service. Saga, Jung Kook, V, Jimin and RM are expected to finish soon.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation
